text: Big Time uses full-size 5-pin DIN MIDI (IN + THRU), default channel 2 — clock it from a Digitakt 2 / Octatrack with a direct DIN cable, no TRS adapter or CB MIDIBox (unlike MOOD/Blooper/Generation Loss).

In Big Time LOOP mode, playback is frozen and STATE/TEXTURE changes are inaudible (hidden digital feedback) — they only take effect on the next OVERDUB pass; VOICING Warm/Analog is the always-live carve.

Big Time LOOP footswitches: TAP LEFT = record then set endpoint then OVERDUB↔PLAYBACK toggle; TAP RIGHT = STOP/reset; HOLD RIGHT = DELETE. (Discrete CCs: REC 104, PLAY 105, DELETE 106, STOP 107, PLAY/DUB toggle 42.)
Big Time STEP does not function in MOD mode or LOOP mode — no footswitch sequence-stepping in either; in MOD, TAP LEFT instead toggles MOTION on/off.

Big Time Env motion: COLOR and FEEDBACK set envelope sensitivity (the follower tracks both input and delay line); with SCALE on, Env becomes a play-triggered step sequencer (Thermae-style), with SCALE off it does continuous pitch bends/dive-bombs.

Big Time MOD mode (3–46ms) is the chorus/flanger/resonator zone, not a delay — factory presets #1 COMPRESSED CHORUS, #2 SLAP/DOUBLE and #5 BROKEN DYNAFLANGE all live here; pull DRY to zero for true vibrato.
Big Time VOICING per-ancestor tone: HiFi = flat (modulation/looping), Focus = gentle band-pass, Warm = ~10k brick-wall (PCM42 X2 character), Analog = ~4k callback to EAE Sending V2.

The PCM42's mis-calibrated limiter inspired Big Time's COMPRESSED / "Blue Mode" state specifically (per EAE) — not the whole COLOR/STATE/TEXTURE architecture; Big Time's limiter is its own analog clipping design in the feedback loop.
The Ursa Major Space Station was a "spiritual influence on Big Time's CLUSTER slider" (scoped to CLUSTER); CLUSTER in MOD mode acts as a thickener/stereo-widener, not a comb/metallic resonator.
Big Time's closest spiritual peer is the Oto Machines BIM (12-bit stereo early-'80s-style delay), not a Strymon/Meris/Empress workstation — same digital-core-wrapped-in-analog thesis.

Big Time's analog limiter is per-channel and lives in the feedback loop with three STATE overdrive levels — push FEEDBACK with Saturated/misbias for musical self-oscillation rather than a digital scream; hold MODE ~2s to panic-reset.
Big Time DRY KILL (remove dry) and DRY CLEAN (strip COLOR from dry only) make it a purpose-built parallel/aux-send and reamp box, not just an inline delay.

TILT EQ sits inside Big Time's feedback loop (CROSSOVER sets the pivot): boosting lows raises loop volume = more/longer repeats; cutting lows shortens the tail — a runaway-control lever, not a static tone knob.
Big Time LOOP buffer trades fidelity for length on TIME: ~48s @ ~44kHz (center), up to ~3.2 min @ ~11kHz, down to ~12s @ ~172kHz.
As of June 2026 no artist-attributed numeric Big Time settings exist anywhere on the web — all reproducible recipes are first-principles builds from the manual, factory presets, and demo transcripts.
